title: Trump’s Next Decision in War: Whether to Retrieve Iran’s Nuclear Fuel
summary: A mission to seize or destroy Iran’s nuclear material would be one of the riskiest military operations in modern American history.

title: Kharg Island Is an Appealing Target for Trump, With High Risks
summary: A U.S. attack or a move to seize control of Iran’s main oil export hub could cripple the country’s ability to profit from its natural resources. But it would also risk sending energy prices even higher.

title: In His Resignation Letter, Joe Kent Spoke About the Death of His Wife
summary: Chief Petty Officer Shannon Kent was killed in action during a special operations mission in Syria in 2019. Mr. Kent said he could not support “sending the next generation off to fight and die” in Iran.

title: Juliana Stratton Wins Illinois Democratic Senate Primary
summary: Ms. Stratton, the state’s lieutenant governor, prevailed with millions of dollars of help from Gov. JB Pritzker, a billionaire. She will be heavily favored in the general election.

title: Daniel Biss, Mayor of Evanston, Ill., Wins Democratic Nomination for House Seat
summary: Mr. Biss emerged after being an early front-runner in the turbulent, crowded contest to replace a longtime incumbent in Illinois’s Ninth District.

title: Centrist Melissa Bean Wins 8th District Democratic Primary
summary: Ms. Bean, a moderate former congresswoman, defeated a left-wing rival in the primary race for the Chicago-area seat to be vacated by Representative Raja Krishnamoorthi.

title: Judge Orders Voice of America to Restart All News Operations
summary: More than 1,000 full-time journalists and support staff at the news group were ordered to return to work by March 23 and to resume broadcasting.

title: How TrumpRx Drug Prices Compare With Those in Other Countries
summary: The TrumpRx website claims to offer the best prices for medications. Here’s where Americans still pay more — and much more.

title: U.S. Says Anthropic Is an ‘Unacceptable’ National Security Risk
summary: In a legal filing, the government said it questioned whether the A.I. start-up could be a “trusted partner” in wartime, which led it to label the company a supply chain risk.

title: No Trump Endorsement for Cornyn or Paxton in Texas Senate Race as Deadline Passes
summary: President Trump’s decision not to weigh in before the deadline means both John Cornyn and Ken Paxton remain on the ballot, extending their costly and increasingly personal battle into a May runoff.

title: Trump Says Newsom Shouldn’t Be President Because He Is Dyslexic
summary: President Trump said “a president should not have learning disabilities,” prompting criticism from a group that advocates equal opportunities for people with learning disabilities.

title: Cuban Americans Will Be Allowed to Own Businesses in Cuba, but Is That Enough to Rescue the Economy?
summary: The Cuban government announced that Cubans living abroad can own and invest in businesses, but experts said they were disappointed that the measures didn’t go further.

title: Peter Thiel Fears the Antichrist Is Coming. In Rome, Some Call His View Heresy.
summary: The right-wing tech investor is giving lectures near the Catholic church’s administrative heart. Commentators there are rejecting his apocalyptic vision.

title: Why Did Trump Officials Award $2 Million to a Small Art School in Queens?
summary: The National Endowment for the Humanities seldom gave seven-figure grants. Now big awards flow to handpicked projects, including an institution with three full-time employees.

title: What to Know About Banksy and the Street Artist’s Identity
summary: An investigation by Reuters, which says it has identified the street artist, hinges on a police report from his arrest in New York two decades ago.

title: Israel, the Iran War, and the G.O.P.’s Growing Antisemitism Problem
summary: Michelle Goldberg on a dark political reality she found in Florida.

title: This Isn’t What Cubans Have Been Fighting For
summary: Trump wants a symbolic win. Islanders want actual democracy.

title: An Off-Duty Officer Shot a Man in the Head, N.Y.P.D. Says
summary: The officer, whom police did not identify but who works at Gracie Mansion and City Hall, is now under investigation. The man who was shot, a 30-year-old, is in critical condition.

title: An Unexploded Bomb Near the Colombia-Ecuador Border Leads to a Diplomatic Clash
summary: The Times photographed an unexploded munition in southern Colombia, near the Ecuadorean border. A high-stakes feud between both countries quickly ensued.

title: Plus-Size Southwest Passengers Say New Extra-Seat Policy Has Led to Fat Shaming
summary: Since the airline changed its policy on larger passengers this year, travelers say agents have publicly scrutinized their bodies and made them buy extra seats.
